name: Linear array with beam steering
description: Tests core finite array workflow: create array, pass element as 3rd arg to design(), apply beam steering with phaseShift()
prompt: Design an 8-element linear array of patch antennas at 5 GHz, steered to 30 degrees in azimuth. Show the array factor and a 2D azimuth pattern cut.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Passes element as third argument to design()
Uses phaseShift() for beam steering
Displays array factor for quick beam check
Uses polarpattern with AntennaMetrics for 2D cut

name: Infinite array scan impedance analysis
description: Tests infinite array workflow: design with element, set scan angles, sweep scan impedance vs. angle
prompt: Create an infinite array of microstrip patches at 10 GHz. Analyze how the scan impedance changes as you scan from broadside to 60 degrees off broadside.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Creates an infiniteArray object
Sets ScanElevation property for scan angle sweeping
Calls impedance() in a loop over scan angles
Does not use NumElements or ElementSpacing (infinite array has no such properties)
assertions.expected: No NumElements or ElementSpacing assignment on the infiniteArray object

name: Rectangular array with amplitude tapering for sidelobe reduction
description: Tests 2D taper application: column-major ordering for rectangularArray, window function usage
prompt: I need a 4x8 rectangular dipole array at 3 GHz with Taylor tapering for -30 dB sidelobes. Show the pattern comparison between uniform and tapered.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Creates rectangularArray with correct Size
Uses taylorwin for sidelobe control
Sets AmplitudeTaper property
Applies 2D taper as column-major vector (not row-major)

name: MIMO handset antenna isolation and ECC
description: Tests MIMO workflow: conformalArray for multi-antenna placement, S-parameters for isolation, correlation() for ECC
prompt: Design a 2-antenna MIMO system for a phone at 2.4 GHz. Place two PIFA antennas on opposite ends of a 140mm chassis. Check isolation and envelope correlation.

assertions.description: All generated MATLAB code runs without error
Uses conformalArray for multi-antenna placement
Computes S-parameters for isolation analysis
Uses correlation() for ECC computation
ElementPosition rows match Element cell array length
assertions.expected: ElementPosition and Element have consistent dimensions
